title: Optimal Face Recognition System using Haar Classifier
publisher: IEEE Smart GENCON 2021 — International Conference on Smart Generation Computing, Communication and Networking
summary: A hybrid face recognition pipeline using Haar Cascade for face detection and LBPH for identity recognition, designed for a Smart Voting System that biometrically authenticates voters before ballot access. The study benchmarks Haar-based approaches against EigenFace, FisherFace, CNN, and PCA-based methods across 14 prior works before implementing and evaluating the Haar+LBPH combination.
findings: Haar Cascade + LBPH selected as optimal hybrid for Smart Voting System authentication. Single-face images yield reliable recognition; multi-face inputs degrade confidence — validating one-voter-at-a-time deployment. LBPH (93.33%) outperformed FisherFace (86.67%) in the closest-domain benchmark. scaleFactor and minNeighbors are the critical tuning levers for Haar detection accuracy. The biometric gate enforces the One Person One Vote policy without human bottlenecks.
